UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user's profile and basic information within the Code Ascent application. Authentication details are managed externally.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

username.description: A unique username chosen by the user for display purposes.

email.description: The user's email address, used for identification (not authentication).

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was last updated.

Course.title: Course
Course.description: Defines a complete programming course, encompassing multiple lessons and topics.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Course entity.

title.description: The title of the course (e.g., 'Python Basics', 'Advanced JavaScript').

description.description: A brief description outlining what the course covers.

difficultyLevel.description: The overall difficulty level of the course (e.g., 'Beginner', 'Intermediate', 'Expert').

programmingLanguage.description: The primary programming language taught in this course.

Lesson.title: Lesson
Lesson.description: Represents a specific lesson or module within a course, containing a series of exercises.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Lesson entity.

courseId.description: Reference to the Course this lesson belongs to. (Relationship: Course 1:N Lesson)

description.description: A summary of the content covered in the lesson.

orderIndex.description: The sequential order of this lesson within its course.

Skill.title: Skill
Skill.description: Represents a specific programming skill or concept that can be mastered, forming part of the visual skill tree.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Skill entity.

description.description: A detailed description of what the skill entails.

prerequisiteSkillIds.description: References to other Skills that must be mastered before this skill. (Relationship: Skill N:N Skill - Self-referencing prerequisite)

Exercise.title: Exercise
Exercise.description: Defines an interactive coding challenge or task for users to complete. This can be a static problem from a lesson or a dynamically generated one.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Exercise entity.

lessonId.description: Reference to the Lesson this exercise belongs to, if it's a static exercise. (Relationship: Lesson 1:N Exercise)

problemStatement.description: The full description of the coding problem the user needs to solve.

initialCodeTemplate.description: The boilerplate code provided to the user to start the exercise.

testCases.description: A string representing JSON data containing test cases (input/expected output) for automated evaluation, or details for AI evaluation.

difficultyLevel.description: The perceived difficulty of this specific exercise.

skillIds.description: References to Skills this exercise helps to master. (Relationship: Exercise N:N Skill)

isGenerated.description: Indicates if this exercise was dynamically generated by the AI.

generatedChallengeId.description: Reference to the GeneratedChallenge entity that created this exercise, if 'isGenerated' is true. (Relationship: GeneratedChallenge 1:1 Exercise)

UserExerciseAttempt.title: UserExerciseAttempt
UserExerciseAttempt.description: Records a single attempt by a user to complete an exercise, including their submitted code and AI feedback.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserExerciseAttempt entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who made this attempt. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N UserExerciseAttempt)

exerciseId.description: Reference to the Exercise that was attempted (can be a static lesson exercise or an AI-generated challenge). (Relationship: Exercise 1:N UserExerciseAttempt)

submittedCode.description: The code submitted by the user for this attempt.

aiFeedback.description: AI-generated feedback on the submitted code, potentially as a JSON string for structured data or plain text.

isCorrect.description: Indicates whether the submitted code passed all checks or was deemed correct by the AI/system.

score.description: A numerical score reflecting the performance on this attempt (e.g., accuracy, efficiency).

submittedAt.description: Timestamp when the code was submitted.

timeSpentSeconds.description: The time, in seconds, the user spent on this attempt before submission.

UserSkillProgress.title: UserSkillProgress
UserSkillProgress.description: Tracks a user's current mastery level and progress for a specific skill.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserSkillProgress entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile this progress belongs to. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N UserSkillProgress)

skillId.description: Reference to the Skill being tracked. (Relationship: Skill 1:N UserSkillProgress)

masteryLevel.description: A numerical value (e.g., 0-100) indicating the user's current mastery level for this skill.

lastPracticedAt.description: Timestamp of the last time the user engaged with content related to this skill.

GeneratedChallenge.title: GeneratedChallenge
GeneratedChallenge.description: Represents the metadata for an AI-generated personalized coding challenge. It links to the actual Exercise entity that was created.

id.description: Unique identifier for the GeneratedChallenge metadata entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile for whom this challenge was generated. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N GeneratedChallenge)

targetWeakness.description: A description of the specific user weakness this challenge was designed to address.

generationParameters.description: A JSON string containing the parameters or prompt used by the AI to generate this challenge.

generatedAt.description: Timestamp when this challenge was generated.

generatedExerciseId.description: Reference to the specific Exercise entity that was dynamically created for this challenge. (Relationship: GeneratedChallenge 1:1 Exercise)

status.description: The current status of the generated challenge (e.g., 'Pending', 'Attempted', 'Discarded').

templateExerciseId.description: Optional reference to an existing static Exercise that served as a template or inspiration for this generated challenge. (Relationship: Exercise 1:N GeneratedChallenge)

definition.description: Stores user profile data. Each document represents a single user's profile and is directly owned by the authenticated user matching `{userId}`. This path ensures private access; `request.auth.uid` must match `userId`.
Stores details of all available programming courses. These documents are globally readable by all authenticated users to allow course browsing.

params.name: courseId
params.description: Unique identifier for a specific course.

definition.description: Contains lessons belonging to a specific course. These documents are globally readable by all authenticated users as part of the course content.

params.name: courseId
params.description: The ID of the parent course.

params.name: lessonId
params.description: Unique identifier for a specific lesson within the course.

definition.description: Defines global programming skills and their prerequisites, forming the basis of the skill tree. These documents are globally readable by all authenticated users.

params.name: skillId
params.description: Unique identifier for a specific skill.

definition.description: Stores static coding exercises that are part of a lesson. These exercises have `isGenerated: false` and are globally readable by all authenticated users. This collection is structurally segregated from user-generated exercises to ensure public access.

params.name: courseId
params.description: The ID of the parent course.

params.name: lessonId
params.description: The ID of the parent lesson.

params.name: exerciseId
params.description: Unique identifier for a static exercise within the lesson.

definition.description: Stores AI-generated coding exercises, which are private and specific to a user. These exercises have `isGenerated: true` and are referenced by a `GeneratedChallenge` document. Access is restricted to the owner (`request.auth.uid == userId`) through the path. No `userId` field is needed in the document itself for authorization due to path-based ownership.

params.name: userId
params.description: The ID of the user who owns this generated exercise.

params.name: exerciseId
params.description: Unique identifier for this specific AI-generated exercise.

definition.description: Records each attempt a user makes on an exercise. Documents include denormalized `userId` for authorization independence and path-based ownership, ensuring only the owner (`request.auth.uid == userId`) can read/write their attempts.

params.name: userId
params.description: The ID of the user who made the attempt.

params.name: attemptId
params.description: Unique identifier for a specific exercise attempt.

definition.description: Tracks a user's mastery level and engagement with specific skills. Documents include denormalized `userId` for authorization independence and path-based ownership, ensuring only the owner (`request.auth.uid == userId`) can read/write their progress.

params.name: userId
params.description: The ID of the user whose skill progress is being tracked.

params.name: skillProgressId
params.description: Unique identifier for a user's progress on a specific skill.

definition.description: Stores metadata for AI-generated personalized coding challenges for a specific user. Documents include denormalized `userId` for authorization independence and path-based ownership, ensuring only the owner (`request.auth.uid == userId`) can read/write their challenges. The `generatedExerciseId` field references an `Exercise` document at `/userProfiles/{userId}/generatedExercises/{generatedExerciseId}`.

params.name: userId
params.description: The ID of the user for whom the challenge was generated.

params.name: generatedChallengeId
params.description: Unique identifier for a specific AI-generated challenge.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure is designed to adhere strictly to the core design principles and mandates, focusing on Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and clear Access Modeling. The primary strategy involves segregating data into globally accessible (read-only for authenticated users) and user-private (read/write for owner) collections. Authorization Independence (Mandate A) is achieved by ensuring that all authorization checks can be performed directly on the `resource.data` or `request.auth.uid` and path parameters, completely eliminating the need for `get()` calls in security rules. For user-owned data, the `userId` is always part of the path (e.g., `/userProfiles/{userId}/...`), and is also denormalized into the document itself where appropriate (e.g., `UserExerciseAttempt.userId`, `UserSkillProgress.userId`, `GeneratedChallenge.userId`) to make authorization intent explicit and robust, even though the path itself provides implicit authorization. This redundancy reinforces clarity and debuggability.

Structural Segregation (Mandate B) is critically applied to the `Exercise` entity. Since an `Exercise` can be either a public, static part of a lesson (`isGenerated: false`) or a private, user-specific AI-generated challenge (`isGenerated: true`), these two types are housed in distinct collection paths: `/courses/{courseId}/lessons/{lessonId}/exercises/{exerciseId}` for static exercises and `/userProfiles/{userId}/generatedExercises/{exerciseId}` for generated exercises. This ensures that documents with different security postures are never mixed in the same collection, simplifying security rules immensely and preventing accidental data exposure. Static exercises are globally readable by authenticated users, while generated exercises are strictly private to the user who owns the `/userProfiles/{userId}` segment of the path.

QAPs (Query/Access Pattern Support - Rules are not Filters) is addressed by this segregation. For globally accessible collections like `/courses` or `/skills`, `list` operations are inherently secure as they grant read access to all authenticated users. For user-specific data, such as `/userProfiles/{userId}/exerciseAttempts` or `/userProfiles/{userId}/generatedChallenges`, `list` operations are naturally filtered by the `userId` in the path, ensuring that users can only query their own data. This structure ensures that no `list` operation will return unauthorized documents, eliminating the need for complex, inefficient, and often insecure filtering within rules.
